heading: Importation of strategic and critical ma­terials
label: 98h–4 Importation of strategic and critical ma­terials
text: The President may not prohibit or regulate the importation into the United States of any material determined to be strategic and critical pursuant to the provisions of this subchapter, if such material is the product of any foreign country or area not listed in general note 3(b) of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States (), for so long as the importation into the United States of material of that kind which is the product of a country or area listed in such general note is not prohibited by any provision of law.19 U.S.C. 1202
notes: References in TextThe Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, referred to in text, is not set out in the Code. See Publication of Harmonized Tariff Schedule note set out under , Customs Duties.section 1202 of Title 19
CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 98h–1 of this title

Amendments1996— substituted “not listed in general note” for “not listed as a Communist-dominated country or area in general note” and “product of a country or area listed in such general note” for “product of such Communist-dominated countries or areas”.Pub. L. 104–2011988— substituted “general note 3(b) of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States” for “general headnote 3(d) of the Tariff Schedules of the United States”.Pub. L. 100–4181987— inserted section catchline and, in text, substituted “The President” for “Notwithstanding any other provision of law, on and after , the President”.Pub. L. 100–180January 1, 1972
